title: NEXTflex™ mtDNA-Seq for blood samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f15b5","text":"The NEXTflex™ mtDNA-Seq Kit is designed to prepare single or paired-end DNA libraries of mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) for sequencing using Illumina® platforms. The procedure isolates mitochondrial DNA from genomic DNA (gDNA) by selective digestion of linear nuclear DNA, followed by fragmentation of mtDNA and library preparation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"hccd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8gsfv","text":"Please see the full manual for additional details.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":15,"length":11}],"data":[]},{"key":"9uk4v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bg74u","text":"(Note: This is the procedure for Blood Samples; for Cell Samples, see this protocol.)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":70,"length":4}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B25BD383468B46F8BD56C88B72BB90BF","url":"http://www.biooscientific.com/Portals/0/Manuals/NGS/5280-01-NEXTflex-mtDNA-Seq-Kit.pdf"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5A598E12B3544A32A5CC7AB50453B2E2","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/NEXTflex-mtDNA-Seq-for-Cell-Samples-dnn5dd"}}}}

This kit provides sufficient reagents for 8 nuclear DNA digestions and mtDNA library preparations. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8bd2a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"85sc7","text":"It is recommended that the mtDNA isolation test (see \"Optional Quality Control of Nuclear DNA Digest\" section) is performed with each isolation. Primers and PCR master mix for this test are provided with the kit. Undigested gDNA may be used as a control (not provided). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6o2pt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"36me0","text":"Reagent Preparation ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f0h12","text":"1. Briefly spin down each component to ensure material has not lodged in the cap or side of tube. Keep on ice and vortex each tube prior to use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9og9t","text":"2. Allow Agencourt AMPure XP Beads to come to room temperature and vortex the beads until liquid appears homogenous before every use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2jfd3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9pgkh","text":"Figure 3A:ÃÂ  Bioanalyzer Gel Image","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2olis","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c59i3","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"8vm8e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2gb9q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7kkon","text":"Figure 3 B: Bioanalyzer Electropherogram Image","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"av3st","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"74rm1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a9fi7","text":"Figure 3A \u0026 3B: Example of a mtDNA Library Size Distribution. Upon isolation the mtDNA was sheared to 150-200 bp, a total of 18 cycles of PCR were performed. 1 ÃÂ¼L of the resulting library was run on an Agilent High Sensitivity DNA chip to verify size. Using a QubitÃÂ® 2.0 Fluorometer \u0026 QubitÃÂ® dsDNA HS Assay Kit, the concentration of the library was determined to be \u003e 10 nM.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"466q3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6a0qf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dvdje","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2sql6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"937mm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2glvg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f8u98","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"frhnd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bbmto","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4rmpg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bsgm9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1vqu6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fhj4g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b2r8q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dn4g9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6nl92","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9lq0c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"C4D5A8E11943492F8D02EBFB32147E6C","height":null,"is_ before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"6enob","text":"Reagent Preparation ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6brqm","text":"1. Briefly spin down each component to ensure material has not lodged in the cap or side of tube. Keep on ice and vortex each tube prior to use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ere0n","text":"2. Allow Agencourt AMPure XP Beads to come to room temperature and vortex the beads until liquid appears homogenous before every use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"678o2","text":"Bioo Scientific strongly recommends that you read the following warnings and precautions. Periodically, optimizations and revisions are made to the components and manual. Therefore, it is important to follow the protocol included with the kit. If you need further assistance, you may contact your local distributor or Bioo Scientific at nextgen@biooscientific.com. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a25ka","text":"• Do not use the kit past the expiration date. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6fbh8","text":"• DTT in buffers may precipitate after freezing. If precipitate is seen, vortex buffer for 1-2 minutes or until the precipitate is in solution. The performance of the buffer is not affected once precipitate is in solution. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d2b5o","text":"• Ensure pipettes are properly calibrated as library preparations are highly sensitive to pipetting error. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ccgak","text":"• Do not heat the DNA Adapters above room temperature. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ftqtv","text":"• Try to maintain a laboratory temperature of 20°–25°C (68°–77°F). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5v1on","text":"• DNA sample quality may vary between preparations. It is the user’s responsibility to utilize high quality DNA. DNA that is heavily nicked or damaged may cause library preparation failure. Absorbance measurements at 260 nm are commonly used to quantify DNA and 260 nm / 280 nm ratios of 1.8 - 2.0 usually indicate relatively pure DNA. Other quantification methods using fluorescent dyes may also be used. The user should be aware that contaminating RNA, nucleotides and single-stranded DNA may affect the amount of usable DNA in a sample preparation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3q2l5","text":"• Vortex the genomic DNA for 10 seconds before quantification. It is critical that the user quantifies the input gDNA correctly. The yield and purity of isolated mtDNA is dependent on the concentration of gDNA used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Briefly spin down each component to ensure material has not lodged in the cap or side of tube. Keep on ice and vortex each tube prior to use.
Allow Agencourt AMPure XP Beads to come to room temperature and vortex the beads until liquid appears homogenous before every use.

For each sample, combine the following reagents on ice in nuclease-free microcentrifuge tubes: %s %s _ μL Nuclease-free Water _ μL gDNA (2 – 4 μg) 5 μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA Buffer Mix 1 5 μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA Buffer Mix 2 5 μL NEXTflex™ Nuclear DNA Digest Mix 50 μL TOTAL Materials Bioo Scientific Supplied BLUE CAP - NEXTflex™ mtDNA Buffer Mix 1, NEXTflex™ mtDNA Buffer Mix 2, NEXTflex™ Nuclear DNA Digest Mix WHITE CAP - Nuclease-free Water User Supplied gDNA isolated from blood in up to 35 μL nuclease-free water Heat block 1.5 mL Microcentrifuge tubes Microcentrifuge Ice Agencourt AMPure XP Magnetic Beads (room temperature) 80% Ethanol, freshly prepared (room temperature) Magnetic rack
Mix well by pipetting.
Incubate in a heat block for 48 hours at 37°C. 48:00:00
Incubate the sample at 70°C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Spin the tubes for 10 seconds to collect contents of the tube. 00:00:10
Add 50 μL of AMPure XP Beads to each sample and mix well by pipetting. undetermined Agencourt AMPure XP Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63880
Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
Place the tube on the magnetic rack at room temperature for 5 minutes or until the supernatant appears clear. 00:05:00
Remove and discard clear supernatant taking care not to disturb beads. Some liquid may remain in the tube.

Wash #1: With tube on rack, gently add 200 μL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each magnetic bead pellet and incubate plate at room temperature for 30 seconds. Carefully, remove ethanol by pipette. 00:00:30

Wash #2: With tube on rack, gently add 200 μL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each magnetic bead pellet and incubate plate at room temperature for 30 seconds. Carefully, remove ethanol by pipette. Ensure all ethanol has been removed. 00:00:30
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and let dry at room temperature for 3 minutes. Do not overdry the beads. 00:03:00
Resuspend dried beads with 36 μL Nuclease-free Water. Mix well by pipetting. Ensure beads are no longer attached to the side of the well.
Incubate resuspended beads at room temperature for 2 minutes. 00:02:00
Place tube on magnetic rack for 5 minutes or until the sample appears clear. 00:05:00
Gently transfer 35 μL of clear sample to a fresh microcentrifuge tube.

To ensure complete removal of nuclear DNA contamination, repeat the digestion with 35 μL eluted material from Step 18. Set up the reaction as follows. %s %s 35 μL Eluted DNA (Step 18) 5 μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA Buffer Mix 1 5μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA Buffer Mix 2 5 μL NEXTflex™ Nuclear DNA Digest Mix 50 μL TOTAL
Incubate for 24 hours at 37°C. 24:00:00

Repeat steps 6-18. If you wish to pause your experiment, the procedure may be safely stopped at this step and samples stored at -20 °C. To continue, thaw your frozen samples on ice before proceeding to section "Optional Quality Control of Nuclear DNA Digest". Section "Optional Quality Control of Nuclear DNA Digest" is an optional quality control check of nuclear DNA digestion. Primers and PCR master mix for this test are provided with this kit. Performing this test with undigested gDNA as a control is highly recommended. If not performing the section test, proceed to section "Fragmentation of mtDNA" for Fragmentation of mtDNA.

For each sample prepare two separate reactions in adjacent wells of a 96-well PCR Plate on ice as described below: a Materials Bioo Scientific Supplied BROWN CAP - NEXTflex™ mtDNA Primer Mix, NEXTflex™ Nuclear DNA Primer Mix GREEN CAP - NEXTflex™ PCR Master Mix WHITE CAP - 6X Loading Dye, MW 100 bp Ready-to-Load Ladder WHITE CAP - Nuclease-free Water User Supplied 2 μL of isolated mtDNA (from sections "Selective Digestion of Nuclear DNA (Cell Samples)" and " Selective Digestion of Nuclear DNA (Blood Samples)") Thermocycler 2% Agarose gel Electrophoresis system 96 well PCR Plate Non-skirted Phenix Research Catalog #MPS-499
Mix well by pipetting.
Apply adhesive PCR plate seal and place in thermocycler for the following PCR cycles: a

Prepare pre-stained SYBR Gold 2% or Ethidium Bromide TAE agarose gel. Mix and pour into gel tray. Load 3 μL of the PCR product mixed with 2 μL of 6X Loading Dye and 7 μL of Nuclease-free Water. Load the samples on the gel along with 6 μL of MW 100 bp Ready-to-Load Ladder. SYBR Gold Thermo Scientific Catalog #S-11494
Run the gel with 1X TAE buffer at 100-120V for 60-120 minutes, or until bands have adequately resolved. 01:00:00

Visualize the gel on UV transilluminator or gel documentation instrument. Check for the presence of a mtDNA band and absence/significant reduction (compared to the control) of nuclear DNA bands to proceed with the subsequent steps (Fig. 2 in Guidelines).

Adjust mtDNA sample volume to 130 μL with Nuclease-free Water. Set pipette to 130 μL and mix well by pipetting. Materials Bioo Scientific Supplied WHITE CAP - Nuclease-free Water User Supplied Up to 40 μL isolated mtDNA (from STEP A1/A2) Covaris AFA microTUBE Covaris Focused-Ultrasonicator System Vacuum concentrator Qubit® fluorometer

Transfer the sample to a Covaris tube. Follow manufacturer's instructions. For example, using the Covaris S2 system, the following parameters will produce fragments of 150-200 bp Peak Intensity – 5 Duty cycle – 10% Cycles per burst – 200 Time – 180 s
Transfer your sample from a Covaris tube to a 15 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Option 1: SpeedVac - following sonication, spin down sample in a SpeedVac for 2 hours at 45°C to reduce the volume of your sample to ≤ 40 μL. Do not let the sample dry down completely. 02:00:00

Option 2: Bead Cleanup – Alternatively a 2X AMPure XP bead clean up can be done as follows: Option 2: Bead Cleanup – Alternatively a 2X AMPure XP bead clean up for NEXTflex™ mtDNA-Seq Kit by Bioo Scientific

After concentrating the sample, Qubit® dsDNA reagents can be used to quantify the DNA concentration. Note that mtDNA accounts for 0.01% to 1% of total DNA and the concentration of isolated mtDNA can be very low. Concentrations typically vary between 0.1 to 1 ng/μL. It is recommended to use all of the fragmented mtDNA in library prep.

For each sample, combine the following reagents on ice in a nuclease-free 96 well PCR Plate: %s %s 40 μL mtDNA (from section "Fragmentation of mtDNA") 7 μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA End Repair Buffer Mix 3 μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA End Repair Enzyme Mix 50 μL TOTAL Materials Bioo Scientific Supplied CLEAR CAP - NEXTflex™ mtDNA End Repair Buffer Mix, NEXTflex™ mtDNA End Repair Enzyme Mix WHITE CAP - Nuclease-free Water User Supplied mtDNA in 40 μL Nuclease-free Water (from section "Fragmentation of mtDNA") 96 well PCR Plate Adhesive PCR Plate Seal Microcentrifuge Ice
Mix well by pipetting.
Apply adhesive PCR plate seal and incubate on a thermocycler for 30 minutes at 22°C. 00:30:00 Adhesive PCR Plate Seal Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #MSB1001

Add 80 μL of AMPure XP Beads to each sample and mix well by pipetting. undetermined Materials Bioo Scientific Supplied WHITE CAP - Resuspension Buffer User Supplied Agencourt AMPure XP Magnetic Beads (room temperature) 80% Ethanol, freshly prepared (room temperature) Magnetic Stand Agencourt AMPure XP Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63880
Incubate sample at room temperature for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
Place the 96 well PCR Plate on the magnetic stand at room temperature for 5 minutes or until the supernatant appears clear. 00:05:00
Remove and discard clear supernatant taking care not to disturb beads. Some liquid may remain in wells.

Wash #1: With plate on stand, gently add 200 μL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each magnetic bead pellet and incubate plate at room temperature for 30 seconds. Carefully, remove ethanol by pipette. 00:00:30

Wash #2: With plate on stand, gently add 200 μL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each magnetic bead pellet and incubate plate at room temperature for 30 seconds. Carefully, remove ethanol by pipette. Ensure all ethanol has been removed.
Remove the plate from the magnetic stand and let dry at room temperature for 3 minutes. Note: Do not overdry the beads. 00:03:00
Resuspend dried beads with 17 μL Resuspension Buffer. Mix well by pipetting. Ensure beads are no longer attached to the side of the well.
Incubate resuspended beads at room temperature for 2 minutes. 00:02:00
Place plate on magnetic stand for 5 minutes or until the sample appears clear. 00:05:00

Gently transfer 16 μL of clear sample to a new well. If you wish to pause your experiment, the procedure may be safely stopped at this step and samples stored at -20°C. To restart, always thaw your frozen samples on ice before proceeding to section "3’ Adenylation".

Combine the following in the 96 well PCR Plate: %s %s 16 μL End-Repaired DNA (from section "Clean-Up") 4.5 μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA Adenylation Mix 20.5 μL TOTAL Materials Bioo Scientific Supplied RED CAP - NEXTflex™ mtDNA-Seq Adenylation Mix User Supplied Thermocycler (set to 37°C) 16 μL of End Repaired DNA (from section "Clean-Up")
Mix well by pipetting.
Apply adhesive PCR plate seal and incubate on a thermocycler for 30 minutes at 37°C. 00:30:00

For each sample, combine the following reagents (in this order) in the 96-well PCR Plate: %s %s 20.5 μL 3’ Adenylated DNA (from the above section) 27.5 μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA Ligation Mix 2.0 μL NEXTflex™ mtDNA Adapter or NEXTflex™ ChIP Barcode 50 μL TOTAL Materials Bioo Scientific Supplied PURPLE CAP - NEXTflex™ mtDNA Ligation Mix (Thaw right before use and store immediately after use at -20°C), NEXTflex™ ChIP Adapter User Supplied 20.5 μL 3’ Adenylated DNA (from section "3’ Adenylation") (Optional) NEXTflex™ ChIP Barcodes – 6 / 12 / 24 / 48 / 96 (Cat # 514120, 514121, 514122, 514123, 514124)
Mix well by pipetting.
Apply adhesive PCR plate seal and incubate on a thermocycler for 15 minutes at 22°C. 00:15:00 Adhesive PCR Plate Seal Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #MSB1001

Add 40 μL of AMPure XP Beads to each sample and mix well by pipetting. undetermined Materials Bioo Scientific Supplied WHITE CAP - Resuspension Buffer User Supplied Agencourt AMPure XP Magnetic Beads (room temperature) 80% Ethanol, freshly prepared (room temperature) Magnetic Stand Agencourt AMPure XP Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63880
Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
Place the 96 well PCR Plate on the magnetic stand at room temperature for 5 minutes or until the supernatant appears clear. 00:05:00
Set pipette to 88 μL and gently remove clear supernatant taking care not to disturb beads. Some liquid may remain in wells.
Wash #1: With plate on stand, gently add 200 μL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each sample and incubate plate at room temperature for 30 seconds. Carefully, remove ethanol by pipette. 00:00:30
